name: azure-cost-optimization
description: Identify and quantify cost savings across Azure subscriptions by analyzing actual costs, utilization metrics, and generating actionable optimization recommendations.
capabilities: Analyze Azure subscription costs using Azure Cost Management API
Identify orphaned resources (unattached disks, unused NICs) for immediate savings
Query Azure Monitor for VM and service utilization metrics
Generate prioritized cost optimization reports with actionable recommendations
Validate pricing from official Azure pricing pages
author: microsoft
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 156 potential security issues including external command execution patterns, filesystem access, and hardcoded URLs. After evaluation, all findings are false positives: backticks and PowerShell commands appear in code examples within markdown documentation (not executable code), path traversal sequences are in template examples (not actual filesystem operations), and URLs reference legitimate Azure pricing pages. This is a legitimate Azure cost optimization skill from Microsoft with no malicious intent detected.
valueStatement: This skill analyzes Azure subscriptions to identify wasted spending and provides actionable recommendations to reduce cloud costs. It queries actual usage data, finds orphaned resources, and suggests rightsizing opportunities.
userTitle: Optimize Azure Costs and Reduce Cloud Spending
limitations: Only reads cost and utilization data, does not modify resources
Requires Azure Cost Management Reader and Monitoring Reader roles
Cannot optimize costs for resources without sufficient historical data
Estimated savings calculations depend on pricing accuracy

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Reducing Cloud Spend
useCases.description: A DevOps engineer wants to reduce monthly Azure spending by identifying unused resources and rightsizing over-provisioned VMs. The skill runs a comprehensive analysis and generates a detailed cost optimization report with specific action items.

useCases.title: Finance Team Cost Analysis
useCases.description: Finance team members need visibility into Azure spending across multiple subscriptions. The skill provides actual cost data from Azure Cost Management API, breakdown by resource, and identifies cost allocation opportunities through tagging.

useCases.title: Cloud Architect Optimization Review
useCases.description: A cloud architect performs quarterly infrastructure reviews. The skill generates comprehensive reports comparing current spending against historical data, identifies trends, and recommends Reserved Instance opportunities.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Cost Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the azure-cost-optimization skill to analyze my Azure subscription and find cost savings opportunities. Focus on identifying orphaned resources and areas where I'm overspending.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants a quick overview of potential cost savings

promptTemplates.title: Comprehensive Optimization Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a full cost optimization analysis on my Azure subscription <SUBSCRIPTION_ID>. Query actual costs from the last 30 days, check VM and service utilization metrics, and generate a detailed report with prioritized recommendations including estimated savings.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants a complete analysis with data-driven recommendations

promptTemplates.title: Redis-Specific Cost Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze Redis and Azure Cache for Redis costs in my subscription. Find failed caches to delete, identify Premium tiers that can be downgraded, and generate a Redis cost optimization report.
promptTemplates.scenario: User specifically wants to optimize Redis spending

promptTemplates.title: Rightsizing Recommendations
promptTemplates.prompt: Query utilization metrics for my Azure VMs over the past 14 days and recommend rightsizing opportunities. Compare current VM sizes to actual CPU and memory usage patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants specific VM rightsizing recommendations

bestPractices: Always query actual costs from Azure Cost Management API rather than estimating
Validate pricing from official Azure pricing pages and account for free tier allowances
Save cost query results for audit trail and historical comparison
antiPatterns: Assuming costs without querying actual data from Azure Cost Management
Ignoring free tier allowances that may explain low or zero costs
Executing destructive operations without explicit approval and testing in non-production first

faq.question: Does this skill delete resources?
faq.answer: No, this skill only analyzes costs and generates recommendations. It never deletes resources. All destructive operations require explicit user approval and should be tested in non-production environments first.

faq.question: What permissions are required?
faq.answer: You need Cost Management Reader role, Monitoring Reader role, and Reader role on the subscription or resource groups you want to analyze.

faq.question: How accurate are the savings estimates?
faq.answer: Savings estimates are calculated using actual cost data from Azure Cost Management API and validated against current Azure pricing pages. Actual savings may vary based on usage patterns and pricing changes.

faq.question: Can this skill analyze multiple subscriptions?
faq.answer: Yes, you can analyze specific subscriptions by ID, subscriptions with a particular prefix, or all accessible subscriptions in your tenant.

faq.question: Does this work with Azure government clouds?
faq.answer: The skill uses standard Azure REST APIs which should work with sovereign clouds, but pricing validation may require adjustments for government cloud pricing endpoints.

faq.question: How often should I run this analysis?
faq.answer: Run a comprehensive analysis monthly for tracking trends, and after any significant infrastructure changes. Run targeted Redis analysis weekly if you have many cache instances.
